channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DNC chair candidate calls for greater investment in GOP states
item.description: In the four-point platform that was first seen by The Hill, Skoufis vowed to “dramatically” enhance the party’s Red State Fund if elected chair.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Treasury says Chinese hackers remotely accessed workstations, documents in 'major' cyber incident
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chinese hackers remotely accessed several U.S. Treasury Department workstations and unclassified documents after compromising a third-party software service provider, the agency said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Allman Brothers’ Chuck Leavell details Jimmy Carter’s musical legacy
item.description: "He was such a likable human being," said Chuck Leavell, former Allman Brothers Band member and current Rolling Stones touring keyboardist.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Army National Guard soldier from Indiana dies in non-combat incident in Iraq
item.description: The Indiana National Guard says a 34-year-old soldier serving in Iraq has died in a non-combat incident.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden closes government as 'mark of respect' for late former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: President Biden on Monday signed an executive order closing executive offices of the government for late former President Jimmy Carter's funeral on Jan. 9.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Trump's Vote of Confidence
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Speaker math: Breaking down how many votes Johnson needs to keep his gavel
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) heads to the House floor on Friday in hopes of being formally reelected Speaker. While Johnson has the endorsement of President-elect Trump to remain Speaker, one Republican is pledging to oppose him, and several others are not committing to supporting Johnson.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Smugglers abandon two migrant girls at southern border with note to authorities
item.description: Smugglers left two young children to fend for themselves at the southern border, for them to be rescued shortly after by Texas troopers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter nears the top of America's 'Most Admired Man' list, according to Gallup
item.description: The late president Jimmy Carter frequently made the top 10 of Gallup's "Most Admired Man List," the analytics firm said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A caucus of military veterans seeks to bridge the political divide in a polarized Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The players in this year's Army-Navy football game kept up a long tradition, with each side honoring the other’s school song when the contest ended. It was an acknowledgment that the future could bring moments when the opponents that day are teammates on deadlier fields.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s convincing 2024 victory sets House GOP up for homefield advantage in 2026 midterm elections
item.description: Republicans will work to defend their fragile House majority in the 2026 midterm elections, but thanks to Trump's convincing 2024 victory, the GOP will enjoy some home-field advantage in key congressional races
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2028 Watch: Here are the Democrats who may eventually jump into the next White House race
item.description: Here is a very early look at the Democratic Party politicians considered to be potential 2028 presidential contenders in the next race for the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former President Jimmy Carter remembered and praised as a humanitarian around the world
item.description: Jimmy Carter, the 39th president of the United States, died at the age of 100 on Sunday and world leaders across the globe are expressing their condolences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Political world offers condolences for Jimmy Carter
item.description: Members of the political world offered condolences and warm wishes for the family of former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday afternoon. They honored Carter’s decades of achievement and service to the country. Carter, who turned 100 earlier this year, died peacefully in Palins, Ga.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden takes jab at Trump while applauding Jimmy Carter's decency, shares fondest memory with late president
item.description: President Biden held a press conference about former President Jimmy Carter's legacy on Sunday, sharing fond memories with the late statesman while also criticizing Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California and national leaders honor former President Jimmy Carter after his passing
item.description: (FOX40.COM) — On Dec. 29, Jimmy Carter, the 39th president of the United States and a 2002 Nobel Peace Prize winner, died in his home in Plains, Georgia, according to the Carter Center. He was the longest-lived president at 100 years old.
item.dc:creator: Adoreil Ayoubgoulan, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fight over spending stopgap foreshadows funding challenges in new Congress
item.description: The messy government shutdown fight this month foreshadows some of the challenges House Republicans could face next year, as a major test on funding awaits the incoming GOP “trifecta.” Congress narrowly averted a government shutdown last week — but not without a bit of drama.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: How Jimmy Carter's marriage to Rosalynn Carter helped shape his legacy
item.description: Throughout his four-year presidency, the pair presented themselves as a team, with Rosalynn serving as one of his closest advisers and even being a presence in Cabinet meetings.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Former President Jimmy Carter dies at 100
item.description: Carter, who served as the 39th president of the United States from 1977 to 1981, died Sunday in Plains, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Trump appears to side with Musk, tech allies in debate over foreign workers roiling his supporters
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump appears to be siding with Elon Musk and his other backers in the tech industry as a dispute over immigration visas has divided his supporters.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: MAGA civil war erupts between Musk, critics over H-1B visas
item.description: A Trump World civil war has erupted over visas for highly skilled workers, with the president-elect’s new tech industry allies like Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy on one side and the anti-immigration MAGA base on the other.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Janet Yellen tells Congress US could hit debt limit in mid-January
item.description: "Treasury expects to hit the statutory debt ceiling between January 14 and January 23," Yellen wrote in a letter addressed to House and Senate leadership, at which point extraordinary measures would be used to prevent the government from breaching the nation's debt ceiling — which has been suspended
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Baja to lease warehouse space to house deported migrants
item.description: He also stated the warehouse location will have easier access from the ports of entry where migrants might be deported instead of having to bus them to several areas in the city.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Kay Granger revelation punctuates increasing scrutiny of aging officials
item.description: The revelation that Rep. Kay Granger (R-Texas) has been living in an assisted living facility is fueling scrutiny of aging public officials, highlighting the shifting norms surrounding lawmaker health, seniority, and for how long it is appropriate to hang on to power.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Getting too close to first responders will soon be a crime in Florida
item.description: Florida will soon have a so-called “Halo Law” meant to protect first responders. The new law, starting in January, creates a 25 feet buffer zone around law enforcement officers, firefighters, and emergency medical crews.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump has pressed for voting changes. GOP majorities in Congress will try to make that happen
item.description: Republicans plan to move quickly in their effort to overhaul the nation’s voting procedures, seeing an opportunity with control of the White House and both chambers of Congress to push through long-sought changes that include voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Opinion: Elon Musk wants to ‘delete’ many Americans' financial lifeline
item.description: It is especially surprising that one of the first federal agencies to come under scrutiny from the incoming administration is one that has returned billions of dollars to many of the same consumers who were counting on leaders in Washington to look out for their wallets.
item.dc:creator: Sharon McGowan, opinion contributor, WFLA, NBC
